title: The Ice Dragon's Sparkly Castle and the Great Ice Festival
summary: A young girl named Izzy and her companion Messer fly to a mountaintop to visit Icicle, an enormous white dragon living in a frozen castle. They bring a thoughtful Christmas gift—a polishing kit and wet wipes—to help the dragon maintain his sparkly home against the mountain's grit. While their friend Demitar hunts for a snack, the group learns about a grand Ice Festival from Rizzo the Rat, who arrives with a magazine detailing the event. The friends share a meal of frozen pecans and fresh fish as they eagerly prepare for the festival where all the ice dragons gather.
audioFilename: Dimitar and Icicle polish the ice castle.m4a

highlightQuote.text: Rizzo the rat does not wear a hat.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Before I go sleep I need to comb Messer.
Oh, before stars and stories you're going to comb Messer? Or do you want to comb Messer while we tell our story?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Here's the mirror, here's the comb for the mermaids, here's Messer's comb.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Okay, once you're done I'll turn out the lights.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Messer, I'm done combing you.
Okay, let's turn out the lights. There's the stars.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Messer. Actually daddy, is Icicle the name of the new dragon? No.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: No. Who... what's the name for the dragon?
Icicle.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Icicle. Let's tell a story about Icicle and Demitar.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Icicle and Demitar. Icicle and Demitar. I'm visiting. I'm going to go visit Icicle and Demitar. So... so I put on my snowpants, jacket and hat and rang Icicle's doorbell, then I flew there with my cape. Ding dong, Icicle.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hello, who is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's... I think I see Demitar with you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yes, Demitar's visiting me right now. Hello!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hi, I'm Izzy. And... come on in! Um, I'm coming in with Messer. Is that okay? Messer won't make a mess.
Well, said Icicle, I like my frozen ice castle to be very neat and clean and sparkly polished. I hope you don't make any messes in my castle, Messer, but you're welcome to visit.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Oh, and Messer says, 'I brought a polishing kit. I brought a polishing kit that... that I made you for Christmas... as a Christmas present.'
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: A present for me? said the enormous ancient white ice dragon.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah, yeah! And it's a polishing kit to polish your ice castle.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Wow, I've never gotten a present. And you know, this side of the ice castle, which faces into the wind and often gets hail and snow and even little bits of pebbles and dirt and grit blown into it, it's getting a little bit yucky and mucky.
Oh, I brought some wet wipes too.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well, that sounds very useful. Would you be willing to help me polish the side of my castle?
Yes, and wipe it after we polish it, and dry it because I brought some dry paper towels to dry it after wet wiping it and polishing it.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And Demitar says, 'Um, well, uh, I'm a little hungry, so you guys, uh, go, uh, enjoy yourselves with all the wiping and polishing and sponging and I'll... I'm going to go get a snack. Bye!' And Demitar flapped up high into the air and circled around once, whoosh! all the way around the castle, and then zoomed off down the mountain.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: For what?
Icicle says, 'Ah, I think he said he was getting a snack. Do you know what Demitar likes to eat?'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I do not.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well, maybe we'll find out if he brings back some to share. Now, let's start...

But my favorite food is pecans. Pecan. Can I share some pecans with you? They were frozen because I bought them at... and Rizzo bought some acorns at the store, he wants to try one. Um, Rizzo came to my house today and he's riding like... and he was riding in his car all the way to... to you. He just drove up the mountain to you.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Is he here right now?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.
Oh, where is he? I don't see him.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Rizzo... Rizzo had to go home to go potty. I see. Rizzo's going potty.
You know, I do have a bathroom here with a very nice toilet, but it is an ice toilet which is a little bit cold on the bum if you're not used to it, you know.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah, Rizzo has a medium potty, you have a bigger potty.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well, mine is, you know, the right size for me, and I'm about the size of a school bus, so it would be about the size of a swimming pool for a little rat, I guess. Let me try one of those frozen pecans.
Oh yeah, here's the pecan. And Rizzo sent in the mail something for an Ice Festival magazine.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: An Ice Festival magazine?

Yes, it's just to tell you about an Ice Festival. I'll read it to you once it's here in the mail. It's here in the mail now, so I'm going to read it to you. 'Ice Festival starts tomorrow. All your friends will be there, even the Ice Dragons. If you want you can come, but please bring a special icy treat.' That's the magazine.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Wow, I didn't know there was an Ice Festival. I didn't even know there were other ice dragons. I'm used to just living here all alone at the top of the mountain in my ice castle, said Icicle. I'm so glad to know there's a festival I can go to. And you said it's tomorrow?
Yes, I said it was tomorrow.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, let's hurry up and finish polishing our castle before Demitar gets back.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Oh yeah, but Demitar's already back. And I'm finished polishing. Are you?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well, I'm almost done. Let me just give another breath of my frozen magic ice breath to it.
Oh, but I'm going to clean that up because you're making such a mess.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I was just going to wipe it after I breathed on it, because that's one way to freeze it again. Well, thanks. Uh, oh hello there, Demitar. Oops. I bumped my head.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Did it need a kiss?
Yes.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Daddy, tomorrow I think we'll go the doctor.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, why is that?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Because I gotta tell them that my head is hurting.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, does it still hurt from your... when you bonked it just now?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.
Oh, I'm sorry to hear that.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: But maybe when I'm done sleeping after breakfast it will be done, and then I will be better soon.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: I think you'll feel better soon. All right, well let's finish up our story. And so Demitar returned with what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A little bucket, and inside the bucket were three tiny fish.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Demitar landed and he said, 'Puff puff puff...'
And what did Icicle say?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And Icicle said, 'Welcome back, friend! What have you got there?'
Fishies, of course, said Demitar. Of course, some little fishies.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, are they for sharing or were you going to eat them all yourself?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, they're for sharing, and I brought Rizzo in this little basket.
Oh, Rizzo's here too? Hello Rizzo.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah, I sure am Rizzo the rat... that does not wear a hat. Rizzo the rat does not wear a hat.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, I'm Icicle and I do not ride a bicycle. And so all the friends shared the little fishies that Demitar had caught, and Icicle blew his ice breath on his portion so he could make a fish-sicle, and he chomped it down with glee. And then they set about preparing for their visit to the Ice Festival. But that is a story for another day. And that's the end of Stars and Stories.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yay! Dad, tomorrow can you do my bedtime also?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Maybe, we'll see.
transcript.speaker: Dad

characters.name: Icicle
characters.description: A titanic and ancient white ice dragon, this towering companion to Dimitar commands the skies with freezing breath and vast, powerful wings. From a shimmering ice palace atop a remote mountain peak, he presides over his crystalline domain with a timeless and frigid majesty, sustained exclusively by a diet of frozen fish.

characters.name: Demitar
characters.description: A winged friend of Icicle who flies down the mountain to catch fish for the group.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

characters.name: Messer
characters.description: Izzy's companion who brings a Christmas polishing kit for the ice castle.

characters.name: Rizzo the Rat
characters.description: A friendly rat who enjoys nature walks, collecting rocks, and exploring the outdoors.

characters.name: Ice Dragons
characters.description: A community of dragons who gather annually for a special festival.

places.name: Ice Castle
places.description: Perched high in the snowy peaks, Icecycle’s lonely fortress is a majestic bastion of solitude constructed from massive blocks of ice. Its jagged, frozen surfaces are meticulously buffed with specialized polishing kits, transforming the mountaintop stronghold into a brilliant, diamond-like spectacle.

places.name: Mountain
places.description: The high, windy location where Icicle resides.

places.name: Izzy's House
places.description: Once the silent harbor of Jinjar’s return and the site of Rizzo and Izzy’s fateful council, this fortified sanctuary now serves as the primary destination reachable via a magical potion. Anchoring the company against the encroaching storm, the hallowed dwelling resembles a quaint hotel of labyrinthine rooms, featuring a celestial starry rug that blankets the ground where heroes once prepared for their departure to the dragon’s castle.
